title: And Then There Were Some: The Gamorrean Guard's Tale

"'''And Then There Were Some: The Gamorrean Guard's Tale'''" by [[William F. Wu]] tells the tale of [[Gartogg/Legends|Gartogg]]—a dim-witted, even by [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]] standards, Gamorrean guard—as he tries to solve a series of murders in [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|Jabba's Palace]]. The short story appeared in ''[[Tales from Jabba's Palace]]'', which was published by [[Bantam Spectra]] [[December]] [[1995]].

==Plot summary==
[[Gartogg/Legends|Gartogg]] is a [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean guard]] in [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|Jabba's Palace]] who dreams of one day serving aboard [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba's]] [[Sail barge/Legends|barge]]. He is disappointed because he is always assigned to patrol the dark back hallways. Gartogg is continuously looking for something important that will promote his status among the other guards. He attempts to curry favor with the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] by solving the mystery behind a rash of [[Death/Legends|murders]] in the palace. Unfortunately, he is too stupid to find the [[Assassin/Legends|killer]] or, indeed, to correctly identify dead bodies when he stumbles across them. The method by which he draws his final conclusion is somewhat erroneous but nonetheless is accurate. Ironically, his idiocy saves his life, as the sail barge trip that [[Standard day/Legends|day]] is doomed.
